title: This Heart That Flutters Near My Heart
content:
This heart that flutters near my heart
    My hope and all my riches is,
    Unhappy when we draw apart
    And happy between kiss and kiss:
    My hope and all my riches, yes!
    And all my happiness.

    For there, as in some mossy nest
    The wrens will divers treasures keep,
    I laid those treasures I possessed
    Ere that mine eyes had learned to weep.
    Shall we not be as wise as they
    Though love live but a day?
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Joyce, titled "This Heart That Flutters Near My Heart", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
